meaning: n.[讀江蟆剰ｱ㋟判(蟋懃ｧ大､壼ｹｴ逕溯拷譛ｬ讀咲黄)
examples: Arab tea, called karak, has cardamom, ginger, milk and sugar.
All the simple recipe involves is beating the mango into pulp, and maybe adding a little milk, cream and cardamom to spice things up.
Small cups of cardamom-infused coffee, juice and biscuits are served immediately.
Cumin, lemon or orange peel, cardamom, dill, clove, aniseed and fennel are also typical.
The maple trees, berberis bushes and larch have fiery autumn leaves, and the cardamom harvest is in full bustle.
